# BATON Universal Router: Full Technical Documentation

This document is optimized for LLMs to generate correct code using the BATON SDK.

## Core Concepts
BATON is an Enterprise Secure, Zero-Trust routing platform. 
The mobile app acts as a dumb terminal. It stores NO API KEYS. 
All intelligence happens on local "Adapter Servers" using the `@baton-router/sdk-node`.

## 1. Installation
Install the SDK via npm:
```bash
npm install @baton-router/sdk-node express cors
```

## 2. Basic Server Setup (BatonRouter)
To connect the BATON mobile app to an LLM, you must spin up an Express server and initialize a `BatonRouter`.

```javascript
import express from 'express';
import cors from 'cors';
import { BatonRouter, OpenAIAdapter } from '@baton-router/sdk-node';

const app = express();
app.use(cors());
app.use(express.json());

// Initialize Router
const router = new BatonRouter({
    verificationToken: 'YOUR_SECRET_TOKEN', // Mobile app sends this for Zero-Trust auth
    corsOrigins: ['*'] 
});

// Configure the AI Adapter
const openai = new OpenAIAdapter({
    apiKey: process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY,
    model: 'gpt-4'
});

// Register the agent
router.registerAgent('gpt4-assistant', openai);

// Expose the webhook for the mobile app
app.post('/baton-webhook', router.handleRequest());

app.listen(3000, () => {
    console.log('BATON Router active on port 3000');
});
```

## 3. Agent-to-Agent (A2A) Routing
BATON can route requests between multiple agents on the same server.

```javascript
import { BatonRouter, Route } from '@baton-router/sdk-node';

const router = new BatonRouter({ verificationToken: 'SECRET' });
router.registerAgent('researcher', geminiAdapter);
router.registerAgent('writer', localLlamaAdapter);

// A2A Handshake: Allow researcher to delegate to writer
router.addRoute(new Route({
    source: 'researcher',
    target: 'writer',
    onIntent: 'draft_report'
}));
```

## 4. Model Context Protocol (MCP)
BATON uses MCP to give agents access to local tools.

```javascript
import { MCPServer } from '@baton-router/sdk-node';

const mcp = new MCPServer();
mcp.registerTool('query_database', async (args) => {
    return { data: "Database results here" };
});

// Attach tools to the agent
router.attachMCP('researcher', mcp);
```

## Prompt Engineering Rules for this SDK:
1. NEVER put API keys in the mobile app. All keys go in the Node.js server environment variables.
2. The entry point for the mobile app is always a webhook URL (e.g., `https://your-server.com/baton-webhook`).
3. Always import from `@baton-router/sdk-node`.
